item_a.name: Greenworks 40V Cordless Lawn Mower
item_a.image_alt: Greenworks 40V cordless electric lawn mower with grass collection bag on lawn
pros: Lightweight design at 37 lbs makes maneuvering easy
Dual battery port allows extended runtime up to 70 minutes
Quieter operation at 75 dB compared to gas mowers
cons: Smaller 16-inch or 20-inch cutting deck options limit efficiency on larger lawns
Battery and charger often sold separately increases initial cost
Fewer attachment and accessory options in ecosystem
item_a.best_for: Homeowners with small to medium yards who prioritize lightweight design and battery compatibility across multiple tools.

item_b.name: Ryobi 40V Brushless Lawn Mower
item_b.image_alt: Ryobi 40V brushless cordless lawn mower with self-propelled feature on residential lawn
pros: Larger 21-inch cutting deck covers more ground per pass
Extensive ONE+ battery system compatible with 300+ tools
Self-propelled models available for easier operation on slopes
Brushless motor provides 20% more runtime and power
cons: Heavier at 48-58 lbs depending on model
3-year tool warranty is shorter than Greenworks
Battery charging time can reach 90-120 minutes for larger capacity batteries
item_b.best_for: DIY enthusiasts with medium to large yards who want compatibility with a vast tool ecosystem and self-propelled convenience.

comparison_summary: The Greenworks vs Ryobi lawn mower debate centers on lawn size and ecosystem priorities. Greenworks offers a lighter, more maneuverable option with dual battery ports and superior warranty coverage, while Ryobi counters with larger cutting decks, brushless motor efficiency, and compatibility with 300+ tools in their ONE+ system. For small to medium yards prioritizing weight and warranty, Greenworks excels; for larger properties and extensive tool collections, Ryobi's brushless power and 21-inch deck provide better value.
verdict_text: Ryobi wins for most homeowners due to its larger cutting deck, brushless motor efficiency, and unmatched tool ecosystem compatibility, though Greenworks remains the better choice for smaller yards and users prioritizing lightweight operation.
seo_content:
When comparing Greenworks vs Ryobi lawn mowers, you're evaluating two of the most popular cordless electric mower brands that have transformed yard maintenance for millions of homeowners. Both companies have established strong reputations in the battery-powered outdoor equipment market, but they take distinctly different approaches to design philosophy and ecosystem integration.

The Greenworks vs Ryobi debate often starts with cutting deck size. Ryobi's standard 21-inch cutting width matches traditional gas mower dimensions, allowing you to cover approximately 25% more area per pass compared to Greenworks' 20-inch models. For homeowners with lawns exceeding 1/4 acre, this efficiency difference translates to measurably shorter mowing sessions. However, Greenworks counters with a significantly lighter design—their mowers typically weigh 37-40 pounds versus Ryobi's 48-58 pound range—making them notably easier to maneuver around obstacles, lift into storage, or handle on uneven terrain.

Battery strategy represents another crucial distinction. Greenworks incorporates dual battery ports on many models, effectively doubling runtime to 70 minutes with two 4.0Ah batteries installed. This innovative approach addresses range anxiety without requiring expensive high-capacity batteries. Ryobi relies on single larger batteries but leverages brushless motor technology that extends runtime by approximately 20% compared to brushed motors. The brushless design also reduces maintenance needs and increases motor longevity.

When examining Ryobi vs Greenworks from an ecosystem perspective, Ryobi's ONE+ platform offers compatibility with over 300 tools spanning lawn care, power tools, cleaning equipment, and even portable electronics. This versatility makes the initial battery investment more valuable if you plan to expand your cordless tool collection. Greenworks supports 60+ tools—still substantial but more focused on outdoor power equipment.

Warranty coverage tilts toward Greenworks with a 5-year tool warranty versus Ryobi's 3-year coverage, though both offer comparable 2-3 year battery warranties. Price positioning places Greenworks $50-100 lower in comparable categories, though kit configurations vary significantly. Self-propelled functionality, increasingly important for sloped yards or larger properties, appears more consistently across Ryobi's lineup.

For small to medium yards under 1/4 acre where maneuverability matters most, Greenworks delivers excellent value with superior warranty protection and lighter weight design. For properties exceeding 1/4 acre or users invested in expanding a comprehensive cordless tool ecosystem, Ryobi's brushless efficiency, larger deck, and ONE+ compatibility justify the premium pricing and additional weight.

faq.q: Are Greenworks and Ryobi batteries interchangeable?
faq.a: No, Greenworks and Ryobi use proprietary battery designs that are not cross-compatible. Each brand's batteries only work within their respective ecosystems, so you'll need to commit to one platform for tool compatibility.

faq.q: Which brand offers better runtime on a single charge?
faq.a: Greenworks typically offers longer total runtime when using dual battery configuration (up to 70 minutes), while Ryobi's brushless motors provide 40-50 minutes with a single 5.0Ah battery but with more efficient power usage that maintains consistent cutting performance throughout the discharge cycle.

faq.q: Do Greenworks or Ryobi mowers work well for thick or tall grass?
faq.a: Ryobi's brushless motors generally handle thick, overgrown grass better due to increased torque and power delivery. Greenworks mowers perform adequately in normal conditions but may require slower speeds or multiple passes in particularly dense or tall grass situations.

faq.q: Which brand has better availability for replacement parts and service?
faq.a: Ryobi has broader retail availability through Home Depot's exclusive partnership, making in-person support and part replacement easier. Greenworks sells through multiple retailers but may require online ordering for specialized parts, though their longer warranty partially offsets this inconvenience.

faq.q: Can these mowers handle hills and slopes effectively?
faq.a: Ryobi's self-propelled models handle slopes better due to variable speed drive systems and heavier weight providing traction. Greenworks' lighter weight makes pushing easier on inclines but lacks the power-assisted control that reduces fatigue on challenging terrain.

meta_title: Greenworks vs Ryobi Lawn Mowers (2026): Which Should You Buy?
meta_description: Detailed comparison of Greenworks vs Ryobi cordless lawn mowers. We break down cutting deck size, runtime, weight, brushless technology, and ecosystem compatibility to help you pick the right electric mower.
